description:
VeilConfigDefinition is the contents of .veil/veil.json — the
 hand-authored source-side project configuration. The "Definition"
 suffix marks it as a local-file shape; types under registry.proto
 (Kind, Registry, …) are the corresponding published forms.

^(resourceDiscovery)$.description:
Where to find the resource JSON files that make up the project's
 catalog. Used at render time to resolve dependency targets.

paths.description:
Glob patterns identifying resource JSON files. Files that don't
 parse as a Resource shape (overlays, fragments, schemas) are
 skipped silently; duplicate (kind, name) pairs across matches are
 rejected.

^(resourceDiscovery)$.title: Resource Discovery

kinds.description:
Relative or absolute paths to kind definition files. May be empty —
 a project with no kinds is valid (it can still depend on kinds
 published by aliased registries).

registries.description:
Aliased registry sources keyed by alias. The empty-string alias
 (`""`) is the default registry: kind references with no `<alias>/`
 prefix resolve there. Named aliases can be any string and are used
 verbatim in references — declaring
 `"acme": "./vendor/acme/registry.json"` lets resources reference
 its kinds as `acme/<kind>`. The `@`-prefixed convention (e.g.
 `"@scope": "..."` referenced as `@scope/<kind>`) is supported but
 not required — the alias is treated as opaque text. Values are
 paths to compiled registry.json files (or URLs, when the loader
 supports them).

resource_discovery.description:
Where to find the resource JSON files that make up the project's
 catalog. Used at render time to resolve dependency targets.

paths.description:
Glob patterns identifying resource JSON files. Files that don't
 parse as a Resource shape (overlays, fragments, schemas) are
 skipped silently; duplicate (kind, name) pairs across matches are
 rejected.

resource_discovery.title: Resource Discovery

additionalProperties.description:
Variable declares a named input available to overlay regex matches
 (and to hooks via the render context). Lives in both the local
 VeilConfigDefinition and the published Kind (carried verbatim
 through compilation), so it has no Definition suffix — the same
 shape applies in both contexts.

default.description:
Optional default. If omitted, the variable is required at render time.
 Must be a scalar matching the declared type.
default.title: Value

description.description:
Optional human-readable description. Rendered as a JSDoc comment on
 the field in the generated veil-types.ts RegistryVariables interface
 so hook authors get inline documentation at their IDE cursor.

enum.description:
Optional set of permitted values. Only valid for `string` and `number`
 types. When present, the generated TypeScript field becomes a union
 ("a" | "b" | "c") instead of the broad scalar type, and values
 supplied via --var or VEIL_VAR_* are rejected at render time unless
 they match one of the listed entries.

type.description:
Declared type. Must be one of the legal VariableType.Enum values
 (the `unspecified` zero is rejected at load time).
type.title: Enum

additionalProperties.title: Variable

variables.description:
User-defined input variables keyed by name. Values are provided at
 render time via --var flags or VEIL_VAR_<NAME> environment variables.

title: Veil Config Definition
